RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: This is a drop in grade but there was no joy in trials and he's looked limited on the track; all five starts at 5f/6f, so raised significantly in distance here.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Tam Man Wai, Wilson Tam Man Shu & Dr Tam Po Chor
Spotlight: Drops into a Class 5 but he's 0-11 and his two placed efforts have been on dirt over 6f; no closer than 4l in two attempts at 1m.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui
Spotlight: Another class-dropper who has won four times and has never been this low in the weights; hasn't been in great form but may show more here from a handy draw.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Three-time winner here at 6f but he stays 1m and has been competitive this year in Class 4s; final run when he finished last needs forgiving but he can run well with cheekpieces removed.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See
Spotlight: Twice a C&D winner; stuck on a long losing run but has been threatening to put that right; stall 12 isn't ideal but he's one to bear in mind.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan
Spotlight: Multiple C&D winner but hasn't been firing for a while now and finished last in a recent trial.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden but hasn't been far away stepped up to 1m in his last two races, latterly here last week; should have a chance with Zac Purton riding for the first time.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Interesting on his 2l defeat over this far at Sha Tin in April but nothing like so good in his last two races and was only fifth in a recent trial.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has been extremely hard to win with in Hong Kong and others are in better form.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Made all over C&D in November but hasn't really approached that form since.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cheung King Shek, Billy Cheung King Shan Et Al
Spotlight: Has a very underwhelming strike-rate but he's run well on several occasions this year and 1m3f was probably too far the last time; possibilities.

RaceName: Silver Grecian Handicap (Class 5) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 0-20 but has run well in spite of some bad draws and was only 2l away when better boxed last time; however, now he's stuck out in 11.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Dannis Lee Cheuk Yin
Spotlight: Not his first C&D win when edging home here two runs ago (April) but never threatened on his return visit off this 5lb higher mark; beat only one home in a recent trial; one or two others appeal more.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Defied the widest stall over C&D in January and has continued to run well, including from box 10; last two runs were at 5f and this is more his trip; chance.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has finished out the back in all five trials this year so he's an unlikely debut winner.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Frankie Pang Ka Yeung & A Pang Ka Wai
Spotlight: Consistent over 6f since his win here in December and every chance again from stall 3 provided he takes to the new visor (replacing cheekpieces).

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in two starts over 6f at Sha Tin but has finished first (only two ran) and second in subsequent trials.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Well behind in two 6f runs on the track and has since gone 0-6 in trials.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Edwin Fong Yick Chung, Edmund Ho Kwok Shing & So Wai Keung
Spotlight: Fourth over 1m in Australia but has looked a sprinter in Hong Kong, showing best form at 6f; however, last three runs were pretty moderate and his usual eyeshield is now left off.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 4-4 in Brazil but remains winless in Hong Kong despite a sliding mark; all his best form is over further.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Modest 1-26 strike-rate but came second over C&D on last two outings, losing out only by a short head on latter occasion; sports refitted cheekpieces; a possible.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Yet to finish better than seventh after ten Hong Kong starts and beaten in a recent trial.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has the form to feature off this low a mark and didn't get the clearest of runs when beaten 3l last time; very effective over C&D but could be better drawn.

RaceName: Fast Most Furious Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cheng Wan Wai, Sheena Cheng Tsz Yan & Selina Cheng Tsz Ching
Spotlight: Off a much-reduced mark he was a close second over C&D in May and he had an outside stall next time; unfortunately, he's got another now.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: First and eighth in recent trials; all three official wins have come over C&D and he has a kinder draw than on his last visit; he's not handicapped out of it despite top weight.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has competitive form in stronger races than this and lurks on a dangerous mark; he is a hold-up performer so will need some luck if dropped in from stall 2, but needs respecting.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: His recent win was over 1m1f on soft at Sha Tin and subsequent defeats at 1m2f; hasn't offered a lot in just two previous visits to Happy Valley.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Regressive 7yo who has not won since December 2024; some good runs this year but below his best of late.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won quite stylishly over 1m1f at Sha Tin in February but below that level since and yet remains 4lb higher.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won over C&D and at Sha Tin earlier in the year but barely sighted in last three races.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Howard Tai Wai Yin & Yim Hin Keung
Spotlight: Won for the Gosdens in Britain; 0-8 in Hong Kong and very quiet in two runs following an absence.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: A number of good runs on dirt before proving no match for an improver over this trip on turf at Sha Tin 11 days ago; unlikely to be far away.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Three course wins; on a lengthy losing run and needs to find something on recent efforts; often held up, so stall 1 could pose problems in that respect.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: One of the favourites when making the most of a drop into Class 5 level over C&D a month ago, collecting by 2l; back up 7lb and returns to a Class 4, but he's not had all that much racing.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ngai Man, Amy Wang Hong & Doris Ngai Yan Tung
Spotlight: All four wins over 1m/1m1f here and nearly added another three runs back off 1lb higher; it wasn't a bad latest effort, either.

RaceName: Turin Warrior Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 4lb lower than for his win in first-time blinkers here in November; he's gone 0-8 since (cheekpieces back on for last two races) but hasn't been running at all badly.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Kwan Kin Man, Dora Kwan Man Ting & Bryan Kwan Hon Ming
Spotlight: On a career-high mark; only worn eyeshields in last two races; recently runner-up over 7f at Sha Tin but didn't appear to see out the 1m back there last time; stamina concerns for now.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Thoroughly exposed 7yo and beaten fair and square in two races after a lengthy break; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Competitive on dirt prior to finishing a close sixth over 7f at Sha Tin on yard debut; that's something to build on, though he's never raced over this far.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: All three wins have come over C&D, the latest by a neck on his penultimate start (drawn high); went up 5lb for that but it was another sound effort from another dodgy draw last time; respected from box 7.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won in New Zealand but it's proving a struggle in Hong Kong and he was well held in his last three starts; failed to convince on his first run over 1m here last time.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Unplaced all ten Hong Kong starts and will do well to turn things around from stall 12.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Just one piece of worthwhile form in nine starts in Hong Hong; finished stone-cold last in final run over C&D.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Still a maiden but a lightly raced one and has been knocking on the door in recent weeks; nearly got up over 7f at Sha Tin last time and he'll probably stay the mile; fifth in a recent trial under Purton, also aboard for that last official race.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Now 0-19 in Hong Kong but keeps sliding down the weights and latest effort can be upgraded.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Billie Lam Chi Fan, Lee Shek Tang Et Al
Spotlight: A winner in Britain but hasn't got going at all since arriving in Hong Kong; best watched.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-6 when in Australia but a down-the-field outsider on all nine Hong Kong starts; very difficult to enthuse over.

RaceName: Sweet Orange Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Yet to be placed but only had six races and some were mildly promising, though not the latest.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Slowly away when running a rare bad race last time; usually consistent in trials and on the track, on turf and dirt; had previously done well to threaten an unexposed winner when drawn wide over 6f on the dirt; considered from stall 4.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Only Hong Kong win was at 5f but his Irish victory was over an extended 7f; has been in a good run of form over the minimum and looks ready for another crack at 6f; draw not ideal but he's still considered.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Had a couple of blips in his seven races but the second time he's been runner-up when beaten under a length over C&D three weeks ago; that was from stall 9, so he could feature from box 11.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Adrian Kwan Chi Keung, Hui Wing Ka & Ho Siu Kong
Spotlight: Both runs at Sha Tin; debut was quite promising over this trip and following defeat was over 7f; that was in early May and beaten in two trials since.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Down the field in 6f/7f handicaps at Sha Tin since nearly springing a surprise on debut.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has twice made all on turf this season (6f/7f) and a good run on dirt two runs back, a surface on which he won a recent trial; perhaps a shade high in the weights but has each-way claims from a good draw; last run was too bad to be true.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Took a few goes to win a trial and hard to warm to on official form, especially from stall 12.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ryan Wong Man Yeung & Roland Wong Ka Yeung
Spotlight: Beaten in trials on either side of bringing up the rear on fourth official start at Sha Tin (6f); no real appeal.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: No wins here but four at Sha Tin, all over 6f; it's well over two years since he has got his head in front but hasn't been far away in last two races, the penultimate one here.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ho Tze Man, Martin Lee Ka Shing & Joseph Tse Lap Bun
Spotlight: Safely held in first three races over 6f and suffered same fate upped to 7f last month.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Only win in Hong Kong came off a much higher mark but over a year ago now (7f); however, he's run some ropey races this year and 6f doesn't look his trip.

RaceName: Star Shine Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 14-race maiden; has a point to prove after latest disappointing run but has a largely consistent record this year and continues to slip down the weights.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed three times in Class 3 this year and went close at Sha Tin when dropped to this level in April (6f, dirt); stymied by stall 14 when back on turf there last month but can be dangerous here.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Lo Kui Lun, Lo Hoe Et Al
Spotlight: Overcame some traffic to lead close home off reduced mark at Sha Tin (6f) 11 days ago; remains well handicapped on early-season form; no surprise if he's in the thick of things again.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Wong Chung Yin, Wong Lai Ngor & Tracy Wong Chui
Spotlight: Displayed sudden jolt of improvement to cause 157-1 shock from stall 12 at Sha Tin in May but suffered heavy defeat there last month and it's hard to know what to expect from him today.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won over 5f here in January and is also suited by 6f; disappointed when dipping back into this grade in May but has much better draw today and might be perked up by first-time visor.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Yet to win this season and too inconsistent to rely up but lost out only by a nose over C&D five weeks ago and has obvious claims if in the right mood again.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Wong Leung Sau Hing & Eddie Wong Ming Chak
Spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong but seemed to benefit from combination drop in trip and first-time blinkers when keeping-on third over C&D three weeks ago; well drawn again; one to consider.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Commendable second on debut at Sha Tin (5f) in May and probably went off a bit too hard before fading into eighth over that C&D a fortnight ago; upped in trip here and remains of strong interest.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Made all from wide stall at Sha Tin in May and both subsequent runs there were respectable but needs to kick on again to overcome this mark.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Belied 94-1 odds when fourth at Sha Tin (5f) in March, his second start, but three down-the-field finishes followed (5f-6f) and he's drawn wide for first run here.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-2 in New Zealand but yet to make a significant impact after 11 Hong Kong starts; his recent C&D runs have been respectable and he's edging down the weights but others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 12-race maiden; shade disappointing when sixth last time and will need to be dropped in from stall 11 today but came fourth on consecutive C&D starts in May/June and has slipped to a tempting mark; not ruled out.

RaceName: Super Oasis Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs James Tien Pei Chun
Spotlight: Unplaced all seven starts, most recently when never-dangerous seventh at Sha Tin last month, and has the outside draw.

RaceName: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
Spotlight: Won this race last year on good to soft (also successful over C&D on good to firm); another decent C&D effort when third in a higher grade in May and creditable third over hurdles since; likely to go well.

RaceName: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
Spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a pair of 1m4f wins in Scotland on good to soft/soft; well below par in three comeback outings this spring and sold out of Iain Jardine's yard for £3,500 last month; all turf starts on good or softer; a watching brief is advised.

RaceName: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
Spotlight: Four-time winner for Ed Dunlop; lost his way in Ireland but bounced back to make a winning start for Tony Carroll at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft; fine on faster) last month; possibly found the run coming too soon when only fifth over 1m at Brighton six days later; probably stays 1m4f and retains handicapping scope on his best form.

RaceName: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
Spotlight: Cheekpieces seemed to have a positive effect early in the year, notably for a 1m2f win at Chelmsford under Freddie Robson; has lost his way since (cheekpieces off here) but is back on the mark he defied at Chelmsford so a revival wouldn't surprise in this uncompetitive race.

RaceName: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner but 0-13 on the Flat; didn't offer a great deal in points and a hunter chase for Tom Fife in the spring; others are preferred unless the betting speaks in his favour starting out for Phil Kirby.

RaceName: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
Spotlight: Winner under both codes in 2024; creditable second over 1m1f at Musselburgh in April but below that level since, including weakening when stepped back up to around 1m4f last time.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to three hurdle/chase winners at up to 2m5f; reached the frame in three Irish points before joining Olly Murphy for £15,000 in May; returns after wind surgery and she needs a close look on rules debut; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to Scottish National winner Macdermott; tailed off in a Warwick bumper in April but she made an encouraging start over hurdles when a staying-on third at Worcester (2m, good) last month; should benefit from that run and she's open to progress on this step up in trip; in the mix.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to point and 2m3f/3m hurdle winner Ree Okka; best of four runs in Irish points when runner-up in May, shortly before £20,000 sale; held in third of six when favourite in Market Rasen bumper last month but that was a tactical race and she wasn't beaten far; this step up in trip looks a good move on hurdling debut and she's strongly respected for top yard.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-12 but she's finished runner-up in her last three hurdle runs including in a Market Rasen handicap (2m7f, good) last month; this drop back in trip is no problem and she has each-way claims back in a maiden.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-10 but she's shown promise over hurdles and was travelling well when she fell three out in a maiden at Market Rasen (2m6f, good) ten days ago; still has potential in this sphere and she's not ruled out back in trip.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown ability over hurdles but she's struggled in her last three runs including when pulled up in a handicap at Market Rasen (2m5f, good) last month; overall record is now 0-9 and others are more convincing.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 and she finished tailed off in a Southwell bumper (2m, good) on debut last March; now goes hurdling after a long absence and she can only be watched.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 5yo mare who raced in two bumpers at Newcastle for Paul Robson, winning comfortably on AW then well beaten on turf; 66-1 on Flat/stable debut at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) last month and she finished a remote last of five; has something to prove now switched to hurdling and others are preferred.

RaceName: Broughton Transport The Mane Logistics Choice Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: His form has been up and down this year but he won at Kempton (6f, AW) in March and was back to his best when making all over this C&D (firm) in May; key player if at the top of his game after a 50-day break.

RaceName: Broughton Transport The Mane Logistics Choice Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Made all at Salisbury (6f, good) in May; unplaced on next two starts but he was a close fifth of seven at Ffos Las most recently and he ran well over C&D last August on firm ground; might not be far away.

RaceName: Broughton Transport The Mane Logistics Choice Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: He'd been headed when badly hampered at Newbury recently (not recover) but was without his usual visor, in which he was running really well in defeat previously under Chloe Lyons at up to 5.2f; he should be fine at this trip; the visor returns and he could bounce back with a big run.

RaceName: Broughton Transport The Mane Logistics Choice Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Experienced 3yo who was winning for third time this year when scoring at Brighton (6f, good to firm) in May; short-headed at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago by one who was completing a hat-trick; major contender.

RaceName: Broughton Transport The Mane Logistics Choice Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners
Spotlight: Capitalised on a reduced mark to finally post her first handicap win at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; had just a neck to spare but this 3yo has gone back up only 2lb; respected.

RaceName: Broughton Transport The Mane Logistics Choice Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won on nursery debut over C&D (good to firm) last September and returned to form to win over C&D (firm) two weeks ago on her second stable start; up 3lb for last time; firmly in calculations kept to her favourite venue.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Alexander Yeung Kwok Kai, Edward Yeung Et Al
Spotlight: Smart in his prime but has not fired on any of four starts during light campaign this season; revival needed.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 2-6 when based in Australia but did not get seriously competitive when a 92-1 outsider for last month's Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (7f).

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a C&D win off a mark 3lb higher than today's but this year's form has largely been underwhelming and his wide draw makes things awkward.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Unplaced all five Hong Kong starts; ran quite well when fifth over C&D from stall 12 in December but this is his first start since and he's drawn wide again.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Now 7lb lower than when causing 50-1 shock over C&D in November but has looked out of sorts in recent months; blinkers removed from headgear combination today.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: 25/26 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: 2-8 when based in Australia but a well-beaten outsider on first two Hong Kong starts (both 7f); tries a new trip today.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won over this trip on Polytrack in Ireland last year but has finished down the field on all three Hong Kong starts (7f-1m).

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner in early part of this season and also ran well when second at Sha Tin (1m1f) in May; below form over that trip here last month and drawn much wider today but still makes more appeal than most of these.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho
Spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; yet to make an impact after five Hong Kong starts (7f-1m) and seemingly in need of a drop in grade.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ling Wai Sun & Michael Ling Siu Ming
Spotlight: Won consecutive 6f Class 4 races in February/March and has been placed in this higher grade since; not yet proven over 1m but has good draw and enters calculations.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Yeung Chun Wang, Yeung Ho Wang & Yeung Chung Wang
Spotlight: Placed three times over C&D since C&D third off 5lb higher in January; needs to rebound from last month's below-par 7f run but had a tough draw that day and might be the answer to this puzzle if back on song.

RaceName: Encountered Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: 24/25 Jimmy K H Ting Trainer Syndicate (2)
Spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after three 6f Hong Kong runs and needs this new trip to prompt improvement.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has good pedigree but hasn't shown much on both starts (remote fourth latterly); early days but he needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Second foal of a 5f 2yo winner (RPR 78); wears blinkers on debut; a first ever 2yo runner in Britain for his trainer but worth a betting check starting out in an ordinary event.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50-1, weakened to finish a remote last of seven on 6f York debut but he did lead until under 2f out in a race won by last week's July Stakes winner Inner City Blues; this is a much weaker race and he's worth a second look in the betting with the shorter trip also a possible help.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten around 11l on both 6f starts last month; significant improvement needed.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has hinted at ability on both starts but South West was 2l ahead of him at Leicester latterly.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown speed on both starts, lasting longer when second to a useful sort at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; the drop to 5f is a help if anything; likely to go well.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 80-1 when well held on 6f Carlisle debut last month; needs to have come on a lot.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Improved effort with blinkers added (retained) when 1l third in 6f Leicester seller (good to firm) 11 days ago; a repeat of that form is likely to see him play a prominent role here.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £50,000 yearling by Harry Angel and the first foal of a 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 84); the stable has a useful 2yo to compare her to in last month's ready Hamilton maiden winner Dubai Champion so a market move would be interesting.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in 6f Redcar maiden and 5.5f Wetherby novice (both good to firm) at the start of the summer but two lesser runs since need forgiving (well below par latest); cheekpieces now added.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-17 over hurdles but went close when runner-up at Market Rasen in January and again in a Cartmel seller in May; well below that level on both starts since, including when well held on the Flat 18 days ago; not ruled out if bouncing back.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: W Trenchard, A Coupland, Cheam Marketng
Spotlight: Three-time Flat winner (1m-1m2f; good or quicker) who returns to hurdles after two fair Flat efforts following wind surgery; took a step forward when a promising fourth on handicap hurdle debut at Plumpton (2m, good to soft) in March and remains capable of better in this sphere; leading contender off a 2lb lower mark.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Low-grade Flat winner who has shaped with some promise in two completed hurdle starts, most recently when a 14l third at Hexham (2m, good) in June; needs to take a step forward on handicap debut but receives a handy weight-for-age allowance.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hinted at ability in three novice hurdles and didn't run badly when third on handicap debut, albeit beaten 18l; failed to back that up at Taunton the following month but has since had wind surgery; returns from a break with a tongue-tie added and could have more to offer.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner whose best hurdling effort came with the switch to handicaps when third at Southwell (2m, good) in May, but he failed to match that form when well backed at Warwick nine days later; latest Flat run is best overlooked and he has claims if reproducing the Southwell effort.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 15-race maiden under all codes but has reached the frame on all three occasions since switched to handicap hurdles; down another 1lb and back in trip, but needs to find more to open his account.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2023 bumper winner but absent a long time before pulling up on hurdle debut in December; showed little in his next three runs after another break until a more encouraging 15l third of 12 at Stratford (2m, good to firm) last month on his second handicap start; still needs better.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well held in three maiden/novice hurdles (2m-2m5f); handicap debut offers hope of improvement but she'll need plenty, even with Sean Bowen booked.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Failed to build on a mildly encouraging hurdle debut at Stratford (2m, good) in October in two subsequent starts, but heavy ground may not have suited over C&D in December; returns from a break for his handicap debut but needs a marked step forward.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has tended to race too freely and has been well held in all three handicap starts despite attracting market support, including after wind surgery; should be capable of better and this is a modest contest but he still needs to learn to settle.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Left behind previous efforts when winning at Market Rasen (2m6f, good; hooded) in May after wind surgery, her fourth handicap start, but never figured over 3m there the following month; bought for £3,000 since and, despite the drop in trip not being an obvious plus, she can have a say.

RaceName: Jack Frost Chiller & Freezer Hire Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Flat winner but 0-18 over hurdles and her thee runs this year have offered little encouragement; easy to look elsewhere.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in two of her four novice/maiden starts and she filled the same position on handicap debut at Catterick last Wednesday (5f, good to firm; led); has her limitations but she's a leading contender in this field.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Improved with each of her first three runs, finishing third at Lingfield (6f, AW) on her third outing; not written off but she flopped at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) last time and it remains to be seen if 5f will suit.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up at Brighton (6f, good to firm) in May for Richard Hughes on her second start and a good case can be made on that form; perhaps unsuited by good to soft ground when tailed off on last month's stable debut at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) but has to leave that performance miles behind.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal, with dam a 1m AW winner (RPR 82); last month's debut sixth of 13 at 66-1 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) gives her something to build on but she may be one for further down the line.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 14-1, raced freely in front and faded into fifth of ten on debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last month; open to improvement and represents an in-form yard; could have a big part to play.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Underwhelming at Lingfield (6f, AW) last month but prior to that she wasn't beaten far when sixth of eight at Ripon (6f, good) in May, showing good speed on that second start; the return to 5f could suit; not ruled out.

RaceName: Resolution Canter Cup EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Tailed off at big odds on both starts (6f, good to firm).

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Shaped quite well when sixth of 15 in the competitive Rous seller at York (6f, good; form worked out) and perhaps 7f stretched her when only third at short odds in another seller at Musselburgh since; returns to 6f for nursery debut with first-time cheekpieces added.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Seems to be getting her act together, finishing off well for third in 5f course nursery (good to firm) last Wednesday; that suggests today's longer trip will suit; gets the vote.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Signs of ability in her three qualifying runs at up to 6f (good/good to soft) and won't need to raise her game too much to be very competitive from this opening mark.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Hinted at ability on Beverley debut but has taken two backwards steps since, finishing a distant last of five in Leicester seller (flashed tail) back from wind surgery seven weeks ago; would need to see market support to consider.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Not at best this season but outran 73-1 odds when placed off reduced mark in 7f Group 3 handicap at Sha Tin last month; sure to be suited by this drop back to 6f; dangerous if in similar form again.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed on both starts (once over C&D) since winning over 5f here in April; probably still on a workable mark and ought to make a bold bid under Joao Moreira from stall 1.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Three wins this season (5f-6f); posted sound effort when sixth in 7f Group 3 handicap at Sha Tin last month but finished behind Lucky With You there and remains on the same career-high mark.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Still 7lb higher than for win at Sha Tin in March but has largely been consistent this year and was denied a clear run before keeping on for third at Sha Tin 11 days ago; each-way claims at least.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Group 1 winner in his prime; plummeted down the weights this season but stopped the rot and caused 58-1 surprise over C&D in May (made all), and has since finished fourth off today's mark; Zac Purton keeps the ride; likely contender again.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: The Rotary Club Of Gd-HK-MA-GBA Syndicate
Spotlight: Dual turf winner in Australia but all five local wins have come on dirt; didn't run badly over inadequate 5f last month and has good draw today but still not the percentage call.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Smart at his best; yet to win this season but on much-reduced mark now and came second twice at Sha Tin last month (6f/5f); cant be ruled out.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong
Spotlight: Six-time C&D winner; sure to be suited by return to this C&D after down-the-field finish over 5f at Sha Tin last month but others look better handicapped.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Gained all four Hong Kong wins over C&D; needs a career-best performance to overcome current mark but ran on well for fourth over 5f at Sha Tin recently and is now back over his optimum trip.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Led close home to exploit a reduced mark seven weeks ago, his eighth C&D success; still very well treated on older form but has wide draw and others have more appealing profiles.

RaceName: Lucky Patch Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Progressive 4yo with 4-9 strike-rate, the latest win gained on soft ground at Sha Tin (6f) in May; below form on latest outing, though, and has double-digit draw to contend with today.

RaceName: Bravo Benefits Celebrations Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed useful form without winning over hurdles for Willie Mullins; left that yard for 64,000gns having finished fourth over fences at Ballinrobe in May but it was an inauspicious stable debut when tailed off in three-runner affair here (2m4f, good) 17 days ago; needs to leave that behind tackling 3m for the first time.

RaceName: Bravo Benefits Celebrations Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fairly useful hurdler who was having her first British start over fences when third of four, beaten 10l, at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last month; her jumping didn't convince that day but maybe returning cheekpieces can help.

RaceName: Bravo Benefits Celebrations Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: S T Racing - The Founders' Syndicate
Spotlight: Two hurdle wins since May and he squeezed in a successful chase debut at Worcester (2m7f, good) in between; latest third over hurdles here (about 2m7f, good) was a solid effort last Tuesday and he's a firm candidate again; due to go up 1lb.

RaceName: Bravo Benefits Celebrations Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Five-time winning hurdler who took a firm step forward from May's chasing debut with a convincing win at Worcester (2m7f) the following month; far from discredited when fifth of 13 in valuable event over C&D (again good) and he's a big player in these calmer waters.

RaceName: Home Group Financial Wealth Management Trophy Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 5.5f course winner (firm) who arrives on the back of a revived 5f win at Chepstow (good to firm); up 3lb for that but has won several times from higher marks and has to be taken seriously.

RaceName: Home Group Financial Wealth Management Trophy Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
OwnerName: Mrs N Davies And Chris Mason
Spotlight: C&D winner (firm) who was third back over C&D on her reappearance in May and ran to a similar level back here next time; produced a career-best effort when winning a Chepstow classified event (5f, good to firm) latest; needs a similar effort to defy this mark back in a handicap but there are plenty of positives, including with her yard among the winners.

RaceName: Home Group Financial Wealth Management Trophy Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: C&D winner; well beaten here in handicaps in May and June; produced her best effort for over a year when winning going away in a Lingfield classified event (6f, good) latest; well treated on her return to this sphere if repeating that effort but it's hard to predict if she will.

RaceName: Home Group Financial Wealth Management Trophy Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two wins over 5f on Polytrack and one at 6f on soft; 6lb lower than when beaten just over 2l here (5.5f, good to firm) in May but hasn't threatened to win for some time.

RaceName: Home Group Financial Wealth Management Trophy Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Standing dish here having recorded seven of his eight wins at the track, three over this trip; best of three efforts for this trainer was the first of them here (5.5f, good) last month; it's hard to rule him out here but he needs to be much better than his last two starts.

RaceName: Home Group Financial Wealth Management Trophy Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Multiple 6f winner, including on fast ground; losing run now at 31 (placed twice at Brighton this year); not written off but has yet to win over this shorter trip.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Not the force of old but won over C&D (good) off 2lb higher in April and last week's second at Epsom (6f, good to firm) confirms he's back in form; bold show likely.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Last two wins have come over 6f at Redcar and he returned to form when third over that C&D (good to firm) in May; well beaten in a big field at York since; first visit to Catterick.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner off this mark on soft ground (also won on good to firm) last autumn; his recent efforts have been no more than respectable but he's nicely handicapped back at venue which suits.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Confirmed 7f course debut promise when seeing off seven rivals in C&D novice (soft) in June; may have bumped into a useful prospect at Leicester (6f, good to firm) since, pulling well clear of the rest; brings obvious unexposed potential to handicap debut.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2yo season with soft-ground maiden win over 6f at Pontefract but hasn't reproduced that form in three handicaps this year, including at Chester on Saturday; has first-time cheekpieces added to the tongue-tie he's worn of late.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well over 6f in nursery at the York Ebor festival and Listed event at Ripon last August; nowhere near that form in three outings since; a two-month break needs to have had a reviving effect.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Loves it here, with all but one of his eight wins coming over C&D; however, failed to build on reappearance when well last of six over C&D at the end of May so his hopes are pinned on a change of stable (has left Stephen Hanlon) sparking a return to form.

RaceName: Le Veinard 2026. Racing And Info Handicap
Spotlight: Successful at Beverley (5f, good) last month and ever better efforts when runner-up twice at Pontefract (both 5f, good) since; dual C&D winner last year so no reason why his run of good form won't continue.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Case Racing Partnership & Anita J Lush
Spotlight: Killarney maiden hurdle winner (2m1f, good) in May last year who made an encouraging start for this stable when third in a Worcester novice in June, but disappointed on handicap debut following a break and hasn't been seen since a heavy fall in November; returns with something to prove.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner (2m-extended 2m4f); has returned from a break with two solid Flat seconds but disappointed over hurdles at Fakenham in May in between; player if back to his best.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who landed a 2m1f chase in May; failed to repeat that form when well held the following month but back over hurdles off a 6lb lower mark and can play a part if back on song.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Back-to-back hurdle wins in May (2m-extended 2m2f) on good or quicker ground, but was most disappointing when a remote third of three on handicap debut at Stratford last month; that effort was too bad to be true and he's not ruled out if bouncing back.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ended a two-year losing run with a strong late surge at Market Rasen (2m, good; last two hurdles omitted) last month in a first-time hood; nudged up only 1lb and likely to be involved again if reproducing that effort.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Readily won over C&D in June on his second start for the stable and, switched to fences, ran well when runner-up off the same mark just eight days later; 7lb higher now but it remains early days for this stable and in handicaps and he could have more to offer.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Simon Tsang Heung Tung & Shirley Tsang Huang Yang
Spotlight: Progressive 5yo who has collected three C&D wins this season; looked as good as ever when second here last month but is drawn wider today and may need to find hidden reserves to overcome current mark.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Holds no secrets from the handicapper but was beaten under 1l when dead-heat third over C&D three weeks ago and will probably be on the premises again.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cheung Ming Man, Cheung Ying Ho Et Al
Spotlight: Nearly caused 65-1 shock over 6f at Sha Tin last month and remains on a good mark but this regressive and inconsistent 9yo was soundly beaten over that C&D 11 days ago; can't be relied upon.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ian Chan Yau Nam & Cyrus Chan Sing Kit
Spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account at Sha Tin in May and was repeatedly denied a clear run when placed from stall 11 off today's mark there 18 days ago; needs to be taken very seriously from the inside berth here.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Suffered dip in form after 5f Sha Tin win on Hong Kong debut in January but his keeping-on fourth over 6f there was a step back in the right direction; considered each-way.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts; did pretty well to finish sixth from stall 12 over C&D three weeks ago but has the same outside draw again; might be one for another day.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ran well from the front and made promising Hong Kong debut when fourth at Sha Tin in May but weakened into midfield over C&D last month and needs a better effort today.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ended last season with a 1m win but has looked out of sorts on all six appearances this term and has left it late in the day to launch his sprinting career.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Shi Ke Min & Frankie Fang Yuan
Spotlight: Dual Class 4 winner at Sha Tin in recent months (7f/1m) but needs to improve again to overcome this raise grade and has wide draw for return to sprinting.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Led close home at Sha Tin (7f) last month, opening his Hong Kong account at the 17th attempt; evidently in good nick but combination of rise in grade and wide draw demands more from him here.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Built upon encouraging stable debut when coming from off the pace for C&D win three weeks ago; back up in grade today but still lightly raced in Hong Kong and might have more to offer for new trainer.

RaceName: Sun Jewellery Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Gained all five wins over 7f, the latest on seasonal debut in September; recent sprint form has been underwhelming and his favourable draw is not enough to tempt today.

RaceName: Novacast Don't Come Last Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 10yo who was tailed off over hurdles a fortnight ago but respectable fourth here (2m1f, good) last month and he won twice here last season (1m6f/1m5f, good to firm/firm); could give a good account.

RaceName: Novacast Don't Come Last Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Dropping down the weights but tailed off at big odds on both starts this season and this 5yo is 0-8; major turnaround needed in first-time headgear.

RaceName: Novacast Don't Come Last Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 16-race maiden (0-11 on the Flat) who failed to threaten over hurdles last time; however, she ran okay in that code two starts ago and was runner-up on the Flat in February (1m6f, AW); not ruled out each-way in a first-time visor (replacing cheekpieces).

RaceName: Novacast Don't Come Last Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Left her three 2yo runs well behind when upped in trip and runner-up on handicap/seasonal debut here a fortnight ago (11.6f, firm; 11-8 favourite); her inexperience was still evident last time and she should have more to offer for a trainer who won this with 3yos in 2023 and 2024; lots to like.

RaceName: Novacast Don't Come Last Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two-time course winner, the latest last September (1m5f, firm); runner-up here on three of her last five starts, including her latest, and this front-runner can make a bold bid.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner from the front last October; mostly creditable efforts in defeat this year and should give it a good shot back at a venue which suits, particularly if he can obtain an uncontested lead.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on turf on first two starts (including good to firm) before making it third time lucky when comfortably seeing off ten rivals at Southwell (7f, AW) last October; subsequently gelded; this is a slightly belated return to the track but he's potentially useful for in-form Ed Bethell.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Wore down a next-time-out winner (pair clear) late on when making it seventh time lucky in 6f Pontefract maiden (good to soft) under Jack Nicholls eight weeks ago; will be equally as effective back at 7f and can be made to look well treated on a few of his runs.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Performed with credit when twice reaching the frame in May; a stumble at the start did not help when only sixth at Thirsk latest but he's winless since 2024 and that wait for a next success may go on unless a change of headgear (first-time visor for regular cheekpieces) gives him a lift.

RaceName: Alfie Flatt 18th Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has returned from a break in good form, building on an encouraging reappearance when adding a third chase win at Warwick (2m4f, good) last month; 8lb higher now and may be vulnerable, but likely to be involved again.

RaceName: Alfie Flatt 18th Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bold-jumping front-runner with seven chase wins, four under Gavin Sheehan; didn't get to the front at Southwell (2m, good) last month and never threatened the winner but shaped as though still in form; remains 7lb higher than for his latest win but could prove hard to catch if allowed an easy lead.

RaceName: Alfie Flatt 18th Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has struggled since winning at Southwell (2m4f, good) in April, most recently when a remote last of nine at Worcester in May; needs to turn things around after a short break.

RaceName: Alfie Flatt 18th Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: The North West National Hunt Racing Club
Spotlight: Ex-French; off the mark at the third attempt for the stable when landing a chase at Bangor (2m4f, good) in May and looked set to follow up there the following month until a couple of late mistakes cost him off a 7lb higher mark; up another 2lb but remains open to improvement.

RaceName: Alfie Flatt 18th Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner but 0-6 over fences and has been out of form on his last two starts, most recently when a 31l last of five at Southwell (2m, good) in June on his return from a break; that run may have been needed but other candidates appeal more.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner in this code at up to 1m4f, including on fast ground; usually held up; has had four starts from 1m-1m2f since a break, running reasonably consistently in defeat; drops 2lb and back again in grade; player.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner at around 1m4f (turf and AW); 3lb lower than her latest success, in February; well below par on her last four starts, including over C&D latest; revival needed dropping in grade.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner who hasn't scored since August 2023; well beaten in his only two starts since September 2024 and looks best watched.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: Triple course winner over 1m-1m2f, including on firm; placed elsewhere over 1m4f; 1lb higher than when winning a 0-60 here last month; claims if repeating that effort but has a bit to prove after his last three starts.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced in seven Flat starts (last ran in this sphere in 2024); triple good-ground hurdle winner at up to 2m1f last summer; reluctant to race when well beaten over hurdles latest; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: Triple course winner, including on firm and over this trip; placed over 1m2f here on his reappearance, when 1lb higher in May; probably too free at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) latest but will be an each-way player if able to settle better.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner on fast ground, including when 2lb higher last August; backed that up with success from 5lb higher at Salisbury (1m2f) next time but has been nowhere near that level since; well beaten here latest and plenty to prove at present.

RaceName: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Charity Handicap
Spotlight: 0-15 and placed just once (1m2f, good to firm); well beaten in a selling handicap (1m4f, AW) on first start for this yard and it's hard to make a strong case for him.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 2-2 for new trainer Robert Cowell when comfortably seeing off eight rivals at Wolverhampton (5f) last week; effectively 7lb higher here but it doesn't look enough to stop him in his current mood.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Respectable third over C&D (good to firm) last month but two lesser Ayr runs have followed, including on Monday.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
OwnerName: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Gained a belated first turf success (18th attempt) when getting up on the line at Musselburgh (5f, good) 15 days ago; his record doesn't make him an obvious one to follow up but he should remain competitive off 4lb higher.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
OwnerName: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner
Spotlight: Latest C&D second on good to firm shows he's in reasonable form; ought to be in the mix again at a course which suits (four wins) but all nine career successes have come on good or softer.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: No impact in sprint handicaps in Ireland for Kieran Cotter but did show ability in maidens last autumn and needs checking out in the betting on first start for in-form Ed Bethell; all turf starts in Ireland came on going softer than good.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
OwnerName: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt
Spotlight: Hasn't finished out of the first three in five starts here, including a win over this trip on good in April; well beaten at Pontefract in first-time blinkers latest but his course record affords him respect with regular cheekpieces also back in place.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Gained his fifth success when seeing off ten rivals over C&D (good) in May; only narrowly denied another at Thirsk (5f, good) last month but below par when 3l third of five at Nottingham latest.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
OwnerName: R R D Saunders And Partner
Spotlight: Back on track with second at Redcar (5f) in April but has finished down the field twice since; bounce back needed.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Third in C&D novice (good to firm) in May but that form has taken several knocks and she's failed to get competitive in two handicaps since.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Hello Cello
Spotlight: Slightly disappointing when fourth of seven here (2m4f, good) last month but her third off today's mark at Warwick (2m5f, good) two starts ago was a good effort and she's unexposed over this trip; could have a say.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Displayed improvement to make winning handicap debut here (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month, coming from off the pace; open to significant further progress over this longer trip and commands respect.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-2 since switched to handicaps and fitted with cheekpieces, the latter win gained from the front at Bangor (2m7f, good) in May; still unexposed over this trip and could still have more improvement to come; big player.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 1-20 over hurdles; bounced back from heavy 2m defeat (wore visor) with respectable fourth at Cartmel (2m6f, good; no headgear) last month and remains unexposed over this sort of trip but others appeal more.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well beaten on first two hurdling starts (2m5f/2m3f) but showed some promise at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) last month, keeping on steadily from the rear for never-dangerous fourth; no surprise if she makes further progress in handicaps.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed twice in spring 2025 (2m4f/2m5f) but generally disappointing since; moves back up in trip after a quiet 2m run last month; cheekpieces (worn for last four races) are removed; others have much less to prove.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well handicapped on old form and will be suited by today's trip/ground but became disappointing last year and made a low-key reappearance in May; revival needed.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returned from break with win at Southwell (3m, good) in April but did not get competitive when sixth at Hexham (the scene of her other win) last month and, even if back on song, might find others in this line-up better handicapped.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but placed twice since upped to staying trips this season, more recently when third at Bangor (2m7f, good) 19 days ago; ought to be on the premises again.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well beaten over 2m on last month's stable debut, after a layoff, and did not achieve a great deal when last of five finishers (albeit not beaten that far) over 2m5f 12 days ago; not yet exposed after only six starts but needs improvement over this new trip.

RaceName: Iglu Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 1-11 but his won was off 1lb higher at Southwell (5f, AW) in January and he ran well for a long way in a Class 4 handicap at Newmarket (fifth of eighth) 11 days ago; headgear is now added and he could make a bold bid on this drop back in grade.

RaceName: Iglu Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Completed a double at Brighton (5.3f, good to firm) last September but he's had only two runs since and has been well held in both of them; been gelded but he has a patchy profile and needs a major revival after another break; withdrawn twice this spring including after getting upset in the stalls here in May.

RaceName: Iglu Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Keen-going sort who proved suited by dropping to 5f when gaining a first win at Lingfield (5f, good) in May; struggled after a slow start at York on his next outing but he bounced back with a close second at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 18 days ago; nudged up 2lb but pulled clear of the third last time and that was behind a major improver who has won won again since; key player.

RaceName: Iglu Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win was in a Hamilton maiden (6f, good) last May but he's well handicapped on his best form and wasn't beaten far from a wide draw at Wolverhampton last time; could go well back in trip and he's not ruled out for yard that won this last year.

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a losing sequence at Kempton (1m) in January but held off higher marks twice since, albeit his latest run may have been needed after a break.

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: Went down by only a head over C&D (firm) in April; things didn't go his way (slightly hampered) when fourth of 11 at Windsor (1m, good to firm) 16 days ago; likely to go well.

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: All ten wins achieved here, dropping to classified company to get his head back in front over this trip (firm) a fortnight ago; part of a strong hand in this finale for Adrian Wintle.

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: Winless since 2022 and nothing like as good now; failed to build significantly on reappearance when sixth of ten over 1m at Chepstow (good) last month and the losing run seems likely to continue

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: The sole 3yo in the line-up; never posed a serious threat back from wind surgery when fifth of seven on 7f Chepstow handicap and turf debut (good to firm) 18 days ago but still early days for a top stable and she shapes as if the step up to 1m could suit.

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: Has largely struggled since landing two wins (including C&D on firm) in the summer of 2024 but did take a step back in the right direction when second of five at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) last Wednesday; claims if he can back that up.

RaceName: Stakes Are Always High Handicap
Spotlight: No show in three qualifying runs (including C&D) but a low-grade handicap does provide her with a more realistic chance.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: HM The King & HM The Queen
Spotlight: Newmarket maiden winner on her sole start last term for whom there were high hopes this year; proved underwhelming when favourite for an Oaks trial at Newbury on her reappearance and was tried in blinkers when tailed off in the Group 2 Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot; now tries cheekpieces on her handicap debut and it's too soon to write her off.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Consistent form in three qualifying races, including when third of eight here (1m2f, soft; acts on quicker) last month; goes up in trip for his handicap debut and looks the solid option.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Best of three previous runs came when fourth of 11 in 1m Southwell maiden (AW; first-time hood) in January but he was 28-1 and beaten 23l when finishing last here (1m1f, soft) on his handicap debut last month; still fairly early days but this new trip will need to spark plenty of improvement.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £12,000 3yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to 2m1f-2m3f chase winner Johnny Og; look for any market clues on this debut.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: In the frame on his last two start in points and confirmed he has ability when fifth of 11 at Market Rasen (good) on his bumper debut ten days ago; that form puts him in the mix and he's probably capable of better again.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten favourite in both Irish points but snapped up for £25,000 in May and made no mistake when winning bumper debut at Worcester (good) last month; uneasy in the market before failing to defy a penalty at Stratford (good to firm) a fortnight ago but she's still a strong candidate.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Falco newcomer whose dam was a 1m2f (2yo) and 1m6f winner (RPR 90), half-sister to useful 1m/10.4f Flat and 2m/2m5f hurdle winner All Annalena; best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Similar form on first and third starts in Irish points, latterly when fourth of 11 (3m, good to yielding) in May; has joined a much respected yard and strength in the betting would aid his cause.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Free Eagle; fourth foal; half-brother to winners God Knows (1m/10.3f; RPR 79) and Fair Damsel (2m1f hurdle); dam 1m3f/1m4f winner (95), half-sister to 7f-1m winner Korbous, out of prolific 6f-1m winner; stable has only the occasional winner in this sphere; market likely to be a good guide to expectations.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000euros foal by Soldier Of Fortune; fifth foal; half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Step Ahead; dam bumper and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle winner, closely related to bumper and 2m/2m3f hurdle winner Elegant Touch, half-sister to bumper, 2m Flat and 2m2f-3m hurdle winner Western Way; trainer has a good record in bumpers and this 4yo hails from a family he knows well; has had wind surgery and tongue-tie already added but needs close market inspection with Harry Cobden booked.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Passing Glance; first foal; dam once-raced half-sister to winners Isle Of Ewe (2m4f hurdle), Charles De Mille (1m) and Sphere (1m4f), out of 1m4f winning half-sister to Prix Du Cadran winner Invermark; stable no stranger to success in this type of event; heed any market clues.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 5,000euros 3yo by Elusive Pimpernel; seventh foal; half-brother to useful 2m hurdle/chase winner Pisgah Pike; dam 2m3f hurdle winner (also placed 1m2f-1m4f Flat; RPR 62), out of French 12.5f winner; yard has a strong record in bumpers; a likely newcomer.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hooded and 10-1 when never a threat at Carlisle (soft) in November; market support would help her enter the picture on this first start since.

RaceName: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 66-1 and beaten 35l on her introduction at Worcester (good) five weeks ago.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Four-time AW winner but just 1-23 on turf; well beaten at Bellewstown on return last time and will have to improve loads to win on these terms.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
OwnerName: Mrs John Murphy & John Pearson & Karl Kirwan
Spotlight: Maiden has shown his best form on the AW; some decent turf runs this term but will have to improve a fair bit on last two efforts to win on these terms.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Sole win came in a Brighton maiden in September 2024; has been regressive over various trips this season including when ninth in a 1m handicap here yesterday; others preferred.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Showed a hint of ability in Flat maidens for Johnny Murtagh last year; didn't take to hurdling for this yard and recent Flat efforts have been poor; looks flattered by a rating of 68.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Got off the mark when winning a Bellewstown claimer over this trip last time; there is more depth to this race but is likely to be involved again.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; was fourth in a 1m2f Curragh maiden for Willie McCreery last year but form deteriorated after; no better on debut for this yard; blinkers need to work.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; won a 1m1f Ayr handicap in May and some decent runs in defeat since including in a Bellewstown claimer behind stablemate Minaun View last time; similar terms today so can be involved.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
OwnerName: Mrs N K Griffin & T Brosnan
Spotlight: Got off the mark when easily winning a Tramore claimer over 1m4f last time; has run well at this track before but likely to find this trip on the sharp side.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Improved from her seasonal debut to get off the mark in a Gowran handicap over this trip last time; is taking on older horses now on unfavourable terms but is in form so that counts in this grade.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a 1m1f Ballinrobe maiden in May; best run since was fourth in a Bellewstown handicap last time; the blinkers worn then are retained but she needs to do more on these terms.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Promise on debut at Naas last year but beaten a long way over 6f at the Curragh on return; needs to improve hugely on that form.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Was progressive in maidens last year without winning; has been regressive in handicaps this term; the blinkers worn last time retained; needs more.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; some promise on debut at Naas but well below that twice since, all on soft ground; has to do much better now tackling faster ground.

RaceName: O'Donoghue Ring Collection Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Some promise in maidens before second (btn 5l) on handicap debut at Leopardstown; found to be coughing post-race after a lesser run there since; in-form yard does well in these events and has a shout on these terms.

RaceName: Aeropak Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 27,000euros yearling; first foal; dam German Listed-placed 8.5f/8.7f winner, half-sister to Group-placed 1m4f Flat and smart 1m7f-2m4f hurdle winner Nemean Lion, out of Listed-placed 1m1f winner; interesting newcomer and he needs a close look for in-form yard.

RaceName: Aeropak Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Kameko filly who has shown promise when fifth over C&D (good to firm) and at Southwell (7f, AW) in the last two of her three runs; needs to find more back on turf but it's still early days and she's a half-sister to eight winners; shortlisted.

RaceName: Aeropak Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ended her three-race juvenile campaign with a much-improved second in a Newmarket novice (7f, good) last October; sets the standard on that form but she's struggled in two handicaps this season and needs a major revival back in a maiden.

RaceName: Aeropak Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; dam 5f/6f winner (RPR 83), half-sister to winners Premier League (6f/7f), Laudate Dominum (7.4f-1m) and Volta Do Mar (7f 2yo), out of 7f 2yo winner; yard 3-57 in maidens in recent years and she could be one for later on.

RaceName: Aeropak Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 9,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to four winners including Mojomaker (5f 2yo; RPR 92), Bona Fortuna (6f; 86) and Bella Kopella (5f-7f inc 2yo/AW; 83); dam unplaced 6f/7f (36), half-sister to 1m Group 2-winning dam of 1m/1m2f Group 1 winner Mount Nelson; yard is 17% in maidens this year and she needs watching in the market on debut.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Wasn't disgraced on his bumper debut here (2m, AW) in December but two lesser efforts in that sphere have followed; switches codes with something to prove.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Proved strong in the market but let his fans down when fourth of six at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in March; contrastingly friendless in the betting on his turf debut at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) in May and he finished tailed off; goes up in trip and has been gelded so worth a market check.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Confirmed she has ability when upped to this sort of trip and chasing home a high-profile filly at Doncaster (good to firm) 19 days ago; looked to need every yard of that test so maybe first-time cheekpieces can sharpen her up a touch; key player.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to four winners, notably Stratum (1m2f-2m5f Flat, RPR 115; useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase), who was hooded when finishing third of three on her debut in warm Windsor novice (1m2f, good to firm) 18 days ago; threatened to get in a bid before weakening that day and she's a candidate to step forward going up in trip on this second start; hood removed.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Elysium (7f 2yo Group 3), Elite Legion (7f 2yo) and Muntahez (1m1f), out of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner; Eur30,000 foal made Eur62,000 as a yearling; by a quality sire and a solid damline; could go well on debut.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: Ran green on debut when fourth over 6f at the Curragh (form franked since); improved on that when a close fourth in the Chesham Stakes at Royal Ascot; should take beating with a repeat of that form.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Nicely-bred sort ran with credit on debut in what looked a strong Curragh 7f maiden; should have taken a step forward since and likely to be more competitive.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Improved nicely from his 7f debut at Down Royal to score in clear-cut fashion at Bellewstown over this trip; needs to take another step forward in this company but a solid effort is likely again.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to winners incl' Across The Nile (1m/8.6f AW; RPR 78) and Feel Good Factor (7f AW; 71); dam Listed-placed 7f winner (inc 2yo; 99); cost Eur32,000 as a foal; related to winners and gets weight from most of today's rivals so can run well.

RaceName: Otex EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: 110,000gns breeze-up buy; sent off favourite on his debut at Beverley (5f, good to firm) two months ago but he raced freely after missing the break and finished well beaten; hood now goes on in a bid to help him settle.

RaceName: Otex EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 150,000euros yearling, 115,000euros breeze-up purchase; Blue Point colt; second foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Indy B (RPR 78); dam runner-up 1m2f in France, half-sister to useful 1m winner Folk Festival, out of Listed-placed 1m1f/1m2f winning sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Parish Hall; one to monitor in the market.

RaceName: Otex EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Stepped up on his debut at Sandown (5f, good) when second of five at Brighton (5.5f, good to firm) three weeks ago, pulling around 6l clear of the third; contender.

RaceName: Otex EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
