Spotlight: Showed plenty on his debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm; 20-1) three weeks ago, keeping on for third after being shuffled back; that form is working out well, with the winner following up and the second also winning since, but this drop to 5f doesn't look an obvious positive (dam's side of the pedigree suggests he may want further).

RaceName: Otex EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £85,000 yearling; shaped encouragingly on his debut at Doncaster (6f, good to firm; 40-1; in cheekpieces) 12 days ago, finishing second of 11 behind one with more experience; likely contender if handling this drop in trip.

RaceName: Otex EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well on her debut at Pontefract (6f, good) in June for Lemos De Souza; failed to beat a rival in the Group 3 Albany at Royal Ascot after a licence switch but should be more competitive returned to maiden company.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 250-1 for both her starts and they were barely rateable; early days but she's likely one for nurseries.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked a shade unfortunate when runner-up on her first two starts but no excuses when collared by an unheralded outsider here (6f, AW; acts on turf) six weeks ago; doesn't look to be doing anything wrong in a finish and she sets a clear standard among those to have raced.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Tasleet; half-sister to winners Hackness Harry (6f AW 2yo; RPR 71) and Fulford Cross (7f AW; 65) for these connections; dam finished fourth (5f) on her only start (2yo, 56), sister to useful 5f/6f winner Primrose Valley, out of multiple 5f winner; market helpful on this introduction.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: She offered something to work on when fourth of ten at Leicester (good) on her debut in June but it was a backward step when well held at Carlisle (also 7f, good to firm) three weeks ago; drops in trip and not without a chance if tapping back into that initial promise.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 3,000gns yearling by Lope Y Fernandez; third foal; half-sister to 1m winner Ballymagreehan (including AW; RPR 67); dam placed 7f AW (66), half-sister to useful 5.7f/6f winner Show Stealer; hooded filly who is the first juvenile runner of the year for her yard.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 12,000gns foal by Kameko whose price dropped to 1,800gns as a yearling; third foal; half-sister to 2m winner Rogue Empire (including AW; RPR 78); dam unraced half-sister to useful 7f 2yo/1m winner Herecomesthesun, out of unraced half-sister to Canadian 1m2f Grade 1 winner Folk Opera; a first ever 2yo runner for her developing stable.

RaceName: Hamways EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ten Sovereigns 9,000euros yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Spoonful Of Sugar (Swedish 6f) and Chispa (Spanish 5f/French 7f 2yo); dam French 5.5f 2yo winner (RPR 84), closely related to 5.5f 2yo Group 2 winner Family One; yard is a respectable 4-20 with juveniles this season and she demands close market inspection on this first go.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Cost Eur50,000 at a breeze-up sale; started 28/1 but ran a solid race in a Naas maiden over this trip on debut; was 2.5l behind Transcript that day and has a bit of work to do to turn that form around.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Started at 50/1 but was a solid third on debut at Fairyhouse; battled on well when a close second at Limerick since; both those runs were over 7f but is bred to stay this trip and is one to consider.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 1m winner Electric Lightning (RPR 65); dam 6f turf 2yo/7f AW winner (91), out of maiden half-sister to a pair of useful winners; Eur10,000 vendor buy-back as a foal; can improve from this debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Best effort in five maidens came when fourth at Naas over this trip last time, first start since being gelded; has less than 1l to make up on Transcript from that run so there shouldn't be much between them today.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Well beaten in both maiden runs and is best watched with handicaps in mind.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Cost Eur75,000 as a yearling and closely related to a Listed winner; only beaten 7l on debut in a Naas maiden over this trip; can improve from that but has 6.5l to make up on Transcript.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Seventh foal; half-brother to winner in Serbia; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 73), half-sister to Italian Listed-placed 5f/6f winner Eva Kant, out of half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Fashionable; Eur5,500 yearling is best watched for future reference on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Decent efforts on first two maiden runs last year for Jessica Harrington but a lesser run when last seen; needs improvement on return/yard debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Tremendous pedigree but was already gelded when making his debut in a Naas maiden over this trip; ran a cracker to finish ahead of several of today's rivals and, with improvement to come, should take a bit of beating today.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Beaten 15l in a Limerick maiden on debut; can improve for that but best watched for now.

RaceName: 4-Head Handicap
Spotlight: Course specialist who has won over 1m here on his last two starts (good to firm; for Taryn Langley), most recently 19 days ago off a 4lb lower mark than today's; needs things to drop right given the way  he's ridden but he can't be dismissed now returned to 7f.

RaceName: 4-Head Handicap
Spotlight: Doubled his tally when winning over C&D in May off a 1lb lower mark than today's but he was below his best behind Gorgeous Mr George here (1m) four weeks ago; drops back in trip with a bounce back needed.

RaceName: 4-Head Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from seven starts came in a Wolverhampton novice (8.5f, AW) in October; she ran well when runner-up in a handicap there in April but well beaten over 1m2f on turf in two starts since and now drops to 7f for the first time since her debut; cheekpieces applied.

RaceName: 4-Head Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 6f maiden here in April but she struggled in handicaps on her next two outings; however, she ran well when second of eight over this C&D last week (good to firm) and has solid claims on that form.

RaceName: 4-Head Handicap
Spotlight: Three of his four wins have been over 6f on the AW but the other came over this C&D (good to soft); latest effort at Southwell (6f, AW) can probably be excused having had a wide trip but others are still preferred; steps back up in trip in a first-time visor.

RaceName: 4-Head Handicap
Spotlight: Thriving at present, winning five of his last six starts, including one over this C&D; those wins have all been in Class 6 company and he is now up in grade under a 4lb penalty (6lb well in) for last week's Brighton win but there's a chance he could get his own way in front.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Sent off 33-1 but she was beaten only a neck when runner-up at Bath (5f, good) on her debut in May and went one better on the AW here (6f) one month later; a subsequent stab at Royal Ascot's Albany is easily overlooked and she brings potential to this handicap debut.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Looked a shade unfortunate to be claimed late when second on her debut at Nottingham in May and there was no disgrace in not being able to handle the subsequent Albany fourth at Goodwood (also 6f, good) a fortnight later; things didn't go her way in 5f AW novice most recently and she's of interest back at 6f in her first nursery.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: It was much her best effort when runner-up at Chester's May meeting (5.5f, good) on her second start but three other outings don't bear comparison; still early days but she enters handicaps with something to prove.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner
Spotlight: Took it up a notch on her handicap debut when making all in Chepstow nursery (6f, good to firm) one week ago; 6lb penalty makes life harder but that was a fairly cosy win and she's likely capable of better; highly respected.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Won Ripon novice (6f, good) on her second start but two heavy defeats have followed, latterly in first-time cheekpieces at Chepstow 18 days ago; new visor needs to help her get back on track on this first crack at a handicap.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Built on her first two maiden efforts over this trip when running away with a 7f Leopardstown maiden last time; the ground was soft that day so different conditions today but has a shout if handling them.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Best run when beaten 5l in a Naas maiden over this trip last time; however, this is highly ambitious and has loads to find in this grade.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Made a winning debut over this trip at Naas and was a close third in a Curragh Group 3 after; was beaten by more than the 1m2f trip there next time and was again below her best at Navan since; has a shout if the tongue-tie and cheekpieces work; looks stable's first-string.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: A big improver this season, winning a valuable Curragh handicap before taking the very competitive Sandringham handicap at Royal Ascot, both over a mile; looks the stable first-string today and can be a big player.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Patrick John O'Brien & Mrs Paul Shanahan
Spotlight: Rapid improver has notched a four-timer between 6.5f and 7f, culminating in a 3l win in a Curragh fillies' premier handicap; has improved 40lb in the weights in that time, is ground-versatile so has to be respected; the trip is a question but promises to stay.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won a 7f Galway maiden in fine style last year and has run well in defeat in Group company three times since over that trip; well beaten in the Irish 1000 Guineas last time; the top-rated in this field but needs to prove she stays a mile.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Beat the mighty Precise in a Fairyhouse 6f maiden on debut last year; missed the rest of last year and the best of three runs this term was a close fourth in a Curragh handicap; has 12l to make up on Green Carrera from the Sandringham h'cap; new yard.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Was a wide-margin winner of a Gowran maiden in April before a solid fourth in a Curragh Group 3; beaten 10l by Precise in the Irish 1000 Guineas since; this is calmer waters and can run well but looks the stable second-string.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won an AW maiden over this trip last November; improved efforts this term in a Curragh Group 3 and the German 1000 Guineas; cheekpieces are added and doesn't have much to find with the best of these so can be a player.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Mrs M V Magnier,Mrs O'Loughlin,Mrs Byrne
Spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk nursery last October and gained another win over the same trip in a Curragh fillies' handicap in May; respectable run in the Sandringham H'cap at Royal Ascot, first in her far side ground; has a bit to find still.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Placed several times in maidens up to this trip including in first-time blinkers at Naas last month; well-held in Listed company the time before; has plenty to find; due to run in the 7.30 here on Monday.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cairn Rouge Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Promise on AW debut last year and showed major improvement to take a Cork maiden on return (7f; sft/hvy); found Group 3 company at the Curragh too tough since and has a bit to find today.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 0-14 and he didn't improve for the step up to 1m4f when fifth of eight at Leicester (good to firm) 20 days ago; drops back down into classified company but others appeal more.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 15-race maiden but he has finished runner-up in classified company on two occasions, including at Brighton (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago; hasn't been with this trainer for long and should go well.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
OwnerName: M L W Bell Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Sole win from 15 starts came in a classified event at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) in February; ran a solid race following a break when runner-up in a handicap here (1m2f) one week ago and he makes the shortlist returned to this longer trip.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has been well beaten in four handicaps this year (1m-1m4f); drops into classified company for the first time but others are preferred.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Dual-purpose performer who ran well when last seen on the Flat in November, finishing third of 12 at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW); however, he's without a success since winning over hurdles two years ago and was well beaten in that sphere when last seen in May; cheekpieces return.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Well beaten in his first three starts and it was the same story on his handicap debut at Epsom (1m2f, good to firm) last week; this step up in trip needs to bring about some improvement but that's possible as his dam won over 1m5f.

RaceName: Doublebase Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Posted a fair effort when sixth of 13 in a classified event at Windsor (1m4f, good to soft) last month but he was well beaten at Epsom two weeks ago, taking his record to 0-7; now back into a classified.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a third win when beating Desert Shadow at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in May and he went down narrowly over that C&D (good; third has won since) four weeks ago; leading claims from an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Two 1m good-ground wins last spring include a defeat of Classic Encounter at Newmarket; creditable fifth of 16 at Meydan in January and although his return to these shores didn't go well when tailed off at Newmarket (raced too freely) in April, he's dipped below his last winning mark; considered.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: 0-10 although he did 'win' at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm; demoted for interference) last August; best run since when second to Classic Encounter at Redcar (1m, good to firm; first-time visor) when last seen in May; gets a weights pull and a form case can be made.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden and novice winner who is yet to win a handicap but last time's third of 14 at Newbury (7f, good to firm; fourth has won since) was his best run of this term; far from ruled out off a career-low mark.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have been on the AW, including in March off 59; close third from a bad draw at Dundalk on Sunday, having 11 days earlier chased home Pete's Dream at Fairyhouse over this trip; has her chance again and good draw plus 5lb claimer booked.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Tipperary winner last summer was only just denied over C&D in May; first-time cheekpieces had no positive effect at Fairyhouse on latest and now discarded.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Two Flat wins came in 2024; recent Fairyhouse run leaves him with a good bit to find but previous Leopardstown third was a solid run; visor tried now, replacing tongue-tie/cheekpieces; interesting jockey booking.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden whose better efforts have come in C&D maidens; both recent handicap runs have been on heavy ground, 1m2f possibly too far at Navan latest; handicapper relenting so not without a chance back on better ground and yard going well.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Belated first win came at Fairyhouse early this month when beating Bella Colombia over this trip on good ground under today's rider; reportedly returned with blood in nostrils but can't be ruled out here off 5lb higher.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner is now 0-16 on turf; encouraging yard debut at Listowel in late May and met trouble in running at Limerick; tongue-tie now tried but wide draw not ideal.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden's best efforts have come in Leopardstown handicaps, including six days ago when staying on fourth (having hung left) off today's mark; not one to rule out but others with stronger claims.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Julianne McNally & Colette McNally & D McNally
Spotlight: Five career wins, last two coming at Listowel; first sign of form this season on latest when never nearer fourth at Roscommon 15 days ago; down to a decent mark so considered.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden reappeared early this month with best run yet in handicaps on yard debut, coming from some way back to finish second at Bellewstown over this trip (good); can go well now from a good draw.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Last turf win came at Naas nearly two years ago (off 63); standout run this term when C&D third (neck off second-placed Jasmine Affanalis) in May, most recently didn't seem to get home at Bellewstown; Colin Keane booking takes the eye.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Tipperary last summer but yet to make an impact in five outings this season.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came at Listowel last summer (off 40); comeback run there in late May should have him straighter but others look more likely.

RaceName: Executive Helicopters Handicap
Spotlight: Breakthrough win came over C&D last October (soft); hooded for somewhat belated seasonal debut here and may just need it.

RaceName: Bazuka Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-time AW winner but he's 1-21 on turf; placed a couple of times this season, including over C&D (good to firm) in April, but he's not easy to predict and was out the back at Doncaster last time; has another switch of headgear and he comes with risks attached.

RaceName: Bazuka Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden but she's been placed in three of her last four handicaps including when a 2l third at Newmarket (6f, good to firm; hood removed) last month; that was in a Class 5 event and she's respected on this drop back in grade.

RaceName: Bazuka Handicap
Spotlight: Promise for Adrian Keatley over the winter but he was expensive to follow for that yard; disappointing in three turf handicaps (6f/5f) for James Owen this season and he needs a transformation with visor now tried.

RaceName: Bazuka Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo who got off the mark when landing a Ffos Las handicap (6f, good) last month; failed to beat a rival in his follow-up bid at Doncaster but he was unable to get a prominent position after a slow start; still has potential and he's a player if he can bounce back.

RaceName: Bazuka Handicap
Spotlight: Showed some promise in her qualifying runs but she started her handicap career with a heavy defeat here (7f, soft) last month; may not have handled that testing ground but she has a bit to prove after that and not sure this drop in trip will suit; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Bazuka Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up off 4lb higher at Kempton (7f, AW) in April but he's been well held in his last three runs and is now 0-11; cheekpieces are removed and visor is now tried.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: Turf winner (good to soft) whose two AW wins came here, latterly over 5f off 1lb lower in March; might have needed it when beaten 6l on his return to turf at Doncaster on Thursday.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: 0-9 (eight on AW) but she went very close at Newcastle (5f, AW) off 2lb higher in December; various headgear (including today's blinkers) has been tried over 6f subsequently and she's shaped like a return to 5f will help.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden, with nine of those runs coming on AW; sole turf outing was a long way below her best but she arrives off the back of two solid thirds; makes first start at 5f and it could well suit.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose mare was gaining her first Flat win in nearly two years when getting up late over 1m2f at Navan on Saturday (first two clear); quick return under mandatory 7lb penalty (upped 10lb in future) and longer trip in her favour.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Last year's win in this race came off 1lb lower (soft); winning hurdler has been running okay on the Flat of late but good bit more needed and draw hasn't been kind.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Winning hurdler for Noel Meade; regressive on Flat in recent years until much more encouraging run at Limerick on Saturday, coming from some way back under 7lb claimer when beaten a head/neck over 1m4f (good); Billy Lee now takes over and major claims off same mark but draw a negative.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Gained fourth career win with 22-1 success at Cork in May in testing conditions; not so good since on faster ground, although fair effort at Navan on Saturday behind Arch Enemy; tries a longer trip here.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Has done plenty of racing since a Limerick win last summer; generally modest form this season, with the exception of a Navan third; others look more likely.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Improved effort on debut for this yard at Navan (1m5f, good) last month when a staying on fourth over 1m5f (good); badly away and didn't handle track at Bellewstown latest so that run forgiven.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo showed some promise in maidens last summer but opening two handicap efforts poor; entitled to come on for last month's comeback run and Colin Keane now booked; 6lb lower but not bred for this longer trip.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Winner over this trip on good ground at Cork last summer for Joe Murphy; tongue-tied on recent yard debut when staying on late into fourth (third has won since) at Tramore, having not handled track; potential improver.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Winner this year over shorter at Dundalk and Fairyhouse and better again over 1m1f at Leopardstown six days ago when beaten a neck off this mark in 22-runner contest, having had every chance; not at best on only previous attempt at this longer trip so bit to prove.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden whose best run for this yard was on seasonal return at Leopardstown when good fourth (1m5f, good); didn't seem to stay longer trip at Bellewstown so should appreciate drop back in distance here.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Has won twice on AW; most recent success in January; 0-15 on turf; however, two runs this term not without promise but plenty more needed; tries a new trip.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Low-grade maiden has posted solid runs in three starts this year, most recently chasing home a 10l winner over this trip at Tramore eight days ago (third has won since, Vervain fourth); should be in the mix and good draw.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo never sighted on last month's Limerick comeback run (1m); big step up in trip and best watched unless market speaks in his favour.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort when second over 1m4f on fast ground for David Marnane in 2024; moderate hurdles form and faded tamely in straight (hung left) on Flat return at Leopardstown last month; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Start Your Free Month Trial At racingtv.com Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to have an impact in handicaps this year, loads to find with some of these on most recent Tramore run.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Trio of Dundalk AW wins this year, including on returning from a short break on Sunday over 1m4f, holding on well after being sent on some way out by today's rider (who is now able to use his claim) in apprentice contest; 0-8 on turf but has to be respected under mandatory 7lb penalty (up 5lb in future).

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time winner in Britain at up to 1m2f (rating peaked at 93); not at the same level for this yard, including over hurdles; handicapper giving him every chance but others preferred.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins, latest win coming over 2m1f (good) at Limerick last June off this mark; respectable return to the Flat at the Curragh last month but good bit more needed.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Boasts a fine AW record (four wins) and gained a first turf victory at Fairyhouse (1m2f, good) in May off 60; soft ground may have been his undoing at Leopardstown following month so respected here back on a sound surface although this much longer trip an unknown.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: In good form last summer, winning at Roscommon (1m4f, good) and placed on four occasions; should be better for last month's return at Fairyhouse and yard in fine form of late.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Winning hurdler is 0-13 on the Flat but several good placings at up to 2m including last month at Ballinrobe when chasing home a ready winner (won again since) over 1m5f; can go well.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Best run for Adrian Murray came when placed in Tipperary 2yo maiden (soft); very light campaign in recent years, soundly beaten over 1m4f at Curragh and Tramore this term; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Gained reward for a string of solid efforts when winning at Navan (1m5f, good) last month and only narrowly failed to follow up at Bellewstown five days later, getting caught on the line over 1m7f after forcing it; up a total of 12lb for those efforts so more needed.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Placed in Naas fillies maiden last summer but didn't progress, well held on handicap debut final start last October for Willie Browne; upped in trip for seasonal/yard debut and watching brief advised.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Appreciated step up to 1m6f at Gowran (good to yielding) for his second handicap start when keeping on for third in May; 2m may have stretched his stamina at Listowel following month; one to consider back to this trip.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo threw away her chance when running wide on turn on handicap debut (apprentice) at Roscommon in May; senior rider takes over.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Good opening efforts in handicaps, staying on late over 1m2f at Cork and raced handier over slightly shorter at Gowran, weakening late on when only beaten 1l into fourth; upped in trip now and contender if he stays.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Three years since her sole Flat win (1m7f, soft) but only narrowly denied over 1m5f at Down Royal last month on similar ground; not as effective on fast ground six days later at the Curragh so ground concerns again here.

RaceName: Kitty O'Se's Seafood And Grill Restaurant Handicap
Spotlight: Much the best of three handicap efforts came on latest when only denied second late on over this trip at Bellewstown (good); bit to find with second-placed Jurality on that form but 3yo looks on the improve and more possible (1lb wrong).
